summary: The diff modifies ClientDetailPage.tsx to guard createClient mutations, show loading/connecting states, and map raw errors to friendly messages. The file-diff summaries reference useQueries.ts and useActor.ts changes, but only ClientDetailPage.tsx is shown in full detail. REQ-88 requirements around useActor.ts caching and stable references cannot be fully verified. REQ-87 and REQ-89 are largely addressed in ClientDetailPage.tsx, though some AC items have gaps.

missing_requirements.requirement: In useActor.ts, ensure the actor is created as soon as an authenticated identity is available and that React Query caches it so subsequent hook calls on the same page receive the same actor instance without re-triggering initialization. The actor query must not return undefined or null once the identity is confirmed; if initialization is in progress it must return a loading state, not an error state.
missing_requirements.reason: useActor.ts is listed as an immutable file and no changes to it appear in the diff. The AC items for REQ-88 (stable actor reference, loading state instead of error, invalidation only on identity change) cannot be confirmed as implemented from the diff.

missing_requirements.requirement: Pressing Create when the actor is not yet ready shows a loading spinner on the button and retries once the actor is available.
missing_requirements.reason: In ClientDetailPage.tsx handleSave, when actorReady is false, the code sets an error message and returns early rather than showing a spinner on the button or automatically retrying once the actor becomes available.

hallucinated_features.description: Client-side BigInt ID generation using a reduce over existing clients with a Date.now() fallback for the first client.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not specify client-side ID generation logic. This is an implementation detail added beyond the stated requirements.
